title: Norwegian Egg Spring Rolls
description: A funky Norwegian dish featuring egg rolls with a cheese-pull effect, perfect for a hangover cure, and dairy-free friendly. This recipe combines the richness of eggs and cheese with the crunch of spring rolls.
instructions: Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk them together with a fork until the whites and yolks are fully incorporated.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped scallions to the skillet and cook until they are softened and fragrant.
Add the shredded cheese to the skillet and stir until it is melted and well combined with the scallions.
Add the egg mixture to the skillet and scramble the eggs until they are cooked through.
Lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface and place about 1/4 cup of the egg mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a little water and fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling.
Fold in the sides of the wrapper and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat the process with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the spring rolls until they are golden brown and crispy.
Drain the spring rolls on paper towels and serve them hot with flambe sauce if desired.
